# aikido_code_repos (Data Source)

Lists code repositories in the Aikido workspace.

## Example Usage

```terraform
data "aikido_code_repos" "all" {}

# Look up a repo by name
locals {
  my_repo = one([for r in data.aikido_code_repos.all.repos : r if r.name == "my-service"])
}

# Link it to a team
resource "aikido_team_resource_link" "repo" {
  team_id       = aikido_team.platform.id
  resource_type = "code_repository"
  resource_id   = local.my_repo.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `filter_branch` (String) Filter repositories by branch.
- `filter_name` (String) Filter repositories by name.
- `include_inactive` (Boolean) Include inactive repositories. Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `repos` (Attributes List) The list of code repositories. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--repos))

<a id="nestedatt--repos"></a>
### Nested Schema for `repos`

Read-Only:

- `active` (Boolean) Whether scanning is active for this repository.
- `branch` (String) The default branch being scanned.
- `connectivity` (String) The connectivity status (e.g., connected).
- `external_repo_id` (String) The external identifier from the source provider.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the repository.
- `name` (String) The name of the repository.
- `provider_name` (String) The source provider (e.g., github, gitlab, bitbucket).
- `sensitivity` (String) The sensitivity level (e.g., normal).
- `url` (String) The API URL of the repository.
